name: PUREX Process
description: Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction process - the dominant nuclear fuel reprocessing technology worldwide using tributyl phosphate (TBP) for selective recovery of uranium and plutonium through multiple chemical separation stages
domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel reprocessing
function_alignment: Direct match - designed for uranium and plutonium extraction from spent nuclear fuel with industrial scale capability
notes: Most established and proven technology, currently operating at required scale worldwide including similar facilities
gap_analysis: Direct match for function requirements - proven technology for uranium/plutonium extraction at industrial scale with multi-fuel capability. Meets all capacity and processing requirements.

name: Tributyl Phosphate (TBP) Solvent Extraction System
description: Key extractant used in nuclear fuel reprocessing for selective separation of actinides, typically dissolved in kerosene diluent for liquid-liquid extraction processes
function_alignment: Core technology component - primary extractant for uranium and plutonium separation with proven industrial application
notes: Essential component of PUREX process, mature technology with extensive operational history
gap_analysis: Core component technology for the required function. Well-established chemistry with proven industrial application for uranium and plutonium separation.

name: PUREX (Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction) Process
description: Established aqueous reprocessing technology using tributyl phosphate (TBP) in kerosene to separate uranium and plutonium from fission products through solvent extraction. Widely implemented at industrial scale including La Hague facility.
function_alignment: Direct - primary industrial method for uranium/plutonium extraction from spent fuel with proven multi-fuel capability
notes: Duplicate of first entry but with specific La Hague reference, confirming applicability to French context
gap_analysis: Perfect alignment with requirements - proven at 1800 tHM/year scale with multi-fuel capability. La Hague reference directly applicable to French jurisdiction.

name: COEX (Co-Extraction) Process
description: Modified PUREX process that co-extracts uranium and plutonium together rather than separately, designed to reduce proliferation risks while maintaining reprocessing capability for MOX fuel cycles.
function_alignment: High - specifically designed for MOX fuel reprocessing with enhanced security features
notes: French-developed technology specifically addressing MOX fuel reprocessing with security enhancements
gap_analysis: Good alignment for MOX fuel processing requirement with enhanced security features. Based on proven PUREX technology but with modifications for proliferation resistance.

name: Enhanced Selectivity Systems with AHA
description: PUREX process enhancement using acetohydroxamic acid (AHA) to improve selectivity in uranium and plutonium separation, with TBP extraction for acid recovery
function_alignment: Process improvement - enhanced selectivity for fissile material recovery in reprocessing operations
notes: Optimization technology that could improve product purity standards and operational efficiency
gap_analysis: Process enhancement that improves selectivity and product purity for fissile material recovery. Builds on established PUREX technology.

name: UREX+ Process Suite
description: Family of aqueous separation processes including UREX+1, UREX+2, UREX+3 variants for selective separation of uranium, transuranics, and fission products with varying degrees of partitioning.
domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - advanced aqueous separations
function_alignment: High - designed for multiple fuel types with flexible separation schemes
notes: Advanced technology offering flexibility but requiring adaptation from US development to French regulatory context
gap_analysis: Meets function requirements with flexible separation schemes for multiple fuel types. Less mature than PUREX but offers enhanced waste management capabilities.

name: Uranium-Lanthanide Co-extraction Systems
description: Direct extraction technology for simultaneous recovery of uranium and lanthanide oxides using tributyl phosphate, applicable to mixed oxide fuel processing
function_alignment: Specialized application - suitable for MOX fuel processing with simultaneous actinide and lanthanide recovery
notes: Specialized for MOX fuels but incomplete solution for full fissile material recovery requirements
gap_analysis: Addresses MOX fuel processing requirement but focuses on uranium recovery rather than comprehensive uranium/plutonium extraction. Specialized application with limited scope.

name: Alternative Diluent Systems (Undecanones)
description: Advanced diluent systems using 2-undecanone as alternative to traditional kerosene for various extraction ligands including TBP, offering improved extraction properties
function_alignment: Process enhancement - improved extraction efficiency for uranium and plutonium recovery
notes: Promising enhancement technology but requires significant development and validation for industrial scale
gap_analysis: Process enhancement component that could improve extraction efficiency but requires integration with established reprocessing systems. Limited industrial experience.

name: Advanced Amide Extractants (DEHBA/DEHiBA)
description: Specialized extractant ligands N,N-di-(2-ethylhexyl)butyramide and N,N-di-2-ethylhexylisobutryamide developed for uranium recovery with improved radiation resistance
function_alignment: Alternative extractant technology - designed for uranium recovery from spent nuclear fuel with enhanced radiation stability
notes: Promising for high-radiation environments but incomplete for comprehensive fissile material recovery
gap_analysis: Addresses uranium recovery but limited evidence for plutonium extraction capability. Enhanced radiation resistance beneficial but incomplete solution for full requirements.

name: Direct Oxide Dissolution in Organic Solvents
description: Alternative reprocessing approach involving direct dissolution of actinide oxides in tributyl phosphate, eliminating aqueous dissolution step and reducing facility requirements
function_alignment: Alternative approach - potentially more efficient process for uranium and plutonium recovery with reduced facility size
notes: Innovative approach requiring extensive development and validation before industrial implementation
gap_analysis: Alternative approach with potential benefits but limited industrial validation. Significant departure from established technology with uncertain scalability to 1800 tHM/year.

name: Pyroprocessing (Electrochemical Reprocessing)
description: High-temperature electrochemical separation process using molten salt electrolytes to separate actinides from fission products, particularly suited for fast reactor fuels and proliferation-resistant operations.
function_alignment: Medium - alternative technology for actinide recovery, limited commercial scale experience
notes: Fundamentally different technology requiring specialized infrastructure, limited applicability to stated fuel types
gap_analysis: Alternative technology for actinide recovery but limited commercial scale experience and different infrastructure requirements. Not optimized for UOX/MOX fuel processing.

name: GANEX (Grouped Actinide Extraction) Process
description: Advanced aqueous process for co-extraction of all actinides (U, Np, Pu, Am, Cm) using novel extractants, designed for Generation IV fuel cycle applications with enhanced waste minimization.
function_alignment: Medium - research-stage process for comprehensive actinide recovery
notes: Advanced research technology not ready for immediate industrial implementation at required scale

name: DIAMEX-SANEX Process Combination
description: Two-stage aqueous separation process: DIAMEX for trivalent actinide/lanthanide extraction followed by SANEX for selective actinide recovery, enabling minor actinide recycling.
domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - minor actinide partitioning
function_alignment: Medium - complementary to main reprocessing for comprehensive waste management
notes: Specialized for minor actinide management, not suitable as primary reprocessing technology for stated requirements
